    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import seaborn as sns\n",
    "from collections import Counter\n",
    "import re\n",
    "import warnings\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')\n",
    "\n",
    "# Make plots look nice\n",
    "sns.set_style('whitegrid')\n",
    "plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (12, 6)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"✅ Libraries imported successfully!\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "1fe695b7-7caf-4df2-a8a0-b752ccdefd82",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Function to Generate Emails"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 16,
   "id": "fba15d58-14f4-4601-88c7-395640e362e3",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Generating sample emails...\n",
      "✅ Generated 1000 emails\n",
      "✅ Saved to data/raw/emails.csv\n",
      "\n",
      "Dataset shape: (1000, 4)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def generate_sample_emails(n_samples=1000):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Generate realistic customer support emails.\n",
    "    In a real project, you'd load actual data instead.\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    np.random.seed(42)  # For reproducibility\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for URGENT emails\n",
    "    urgent_subjects = [\n",
    "        'URGENT: Account locked',\n",
    "        'Cannot access my account!!!',\n",
    "        'Payment failed - CRITICAL',\n",
    "        'Security breach detected',\n",
    "        'Lost all my data - HELP',\n",
    "        'EMERGENCY: Service completely down',\n",
    "        'Account hacked - need immediate help',\n",
    "        'CRITICAL ERROR - Cannot login'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    urgent_bodies = [\n",
    "        'I cannot access my account and I have an important meeting in 1 hour. This is extremely urgent! Please help ASAP.',\n",
    "        'My payment failed and now my account is locked. I need this fixed immediately!!!',\n",
    "        'I think my account has been hacked. All my data is gone. URGENT.',\n",
    "        'The entire service is down for my team. We cannot work. This is critical!',\n",
    "        'I lost access to all my files. Need help NOW!',\n",
    "        'CRITICAL: Cannot login and I have a deadline in 30 minutes!',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for HIGH priority\n",
    "    high_subjects = [\n",
    "        'Feature not working properly',\n",
    "        'Error message when exporting',\n",
    "        'Bug in the dashboard',\n",
    "        'Cannot upload files',\n",
    "        'Integration stopped working',\n",
    "        'Getting error code 500'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    high_bodies = [\n",
    "        'I am getting an error when I try to export my data. Can you help me fix this?',\n",
    "        'The reporting feature stopped working. I need this for my weekly report.',\n",
    "        'Files are not uploading correctly. This is affecting my workflow.',\n",
    "        'Getting an error code when I try to access the dashboard.',\n",
    "        'The integration with Slack is broken. Please look into this.',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for MEDIUM priority\n",
    "    medium_subjects = [\n",
    "        'Question about billing',\n",
    "        'How to use advanced features?',\n",
    "        'Need clarification on pricing',\n",
    "        'Setup assistance needed',\n",
    "        'Best practices question',\n",
    "        'How do I configure this?'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    medium_bodies = [\n",
    "        'I have a question about how the billing works for multiple users.',\n",
    "        'Can you explain how to set up the advanced filters? Thanks.',\n",
    "        'I need some clarification on the different pricing tiers.',\n",
    "        'What is the best way to organize my projects in the system?',\n",
    "        'Could you help me understand how the permissions work?',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for LOW priority\n",
    "    low_subjects = [\n",
    "        'Feature suggestion',\n",
    "        'Feedback on recent update',\n",
    "        'Enhancement idea',\n",
    "        'Would be nice to have...',\n",
    "        'General feedback',\n",
    "        'Suggestion for improvement'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    low_bodies = [\n",
    "        'It would be great if you could add a dark mode option in the future.',\n",
    "        'I have a suggestion for improving the user interface.',\n",
    "        'Overall the product is good. Here is some feedback for consideration.',\n",
    "        'Would be nice to have an export to PDF feature someday.',\n",
    "        'Consider adding keyboard shortcuts. Just a suggestion!',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Create emails with realistic distribution\n",
    "    # Real customer support: 10% Urgent, 25% High, 40% Medium, 25% Low\n",
    "    \n",
    "    emails = []\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate URGENT emails (10%)\n",
    "    n_urgent = int(n_samples * 0.10)\n",
    "    for i in range(n_urgent):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': i + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(urgent_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(urgent_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'Urgent'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate HIGH priority emails (25%)\n",
    "    n_high = int(n_samples * 0.25)\n",
    "    for i in range(n_high):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': len(emails) + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(high_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(high_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'High'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate MEDIUM priority emails (40%)\n",
    "    n_medium = int(n_samples * 0.40)\n",
    "    for i in range(n_medium):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': len(emails) + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(medium_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(medium_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'Medium'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate LOW priority emails (25%)\n",
    "    n_low = n_samples - len(emails)  # Remaining\n",
    "    for i in range(n_low):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': len(emails) + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(low_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(low_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'Low'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Shuffle so they're not ordered by priority\n",
    "    np.random.shuffle(emails)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Re-assign email IDs after shuffling\n",
    "    for i, email in enumerate(emails):\n",
    "        email['email_id'] = i + 1\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return pd.DataFrame(emails)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Generate the data\n",
    "print(\"Generating sample emails...\")\n",
    "df = generate_sample_emails(1000)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Save it\n",
    "df.to_csv('../data/raw/emails.csv', index=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Generated {len(df)} emails\")\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Saved to data/raw/emails.csv\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nDataset shape: {df.shape}\")"
   ]
  },

     "text": [
      "\n",
      "⚠️  OBSERVATION: Class Imbalance Detected!\n",
      "   Urgent emails are only ~10% of the data\n",
      "   This means we need to use 'class_weight=balanced' when training\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Bar chart\n",
    "colors = {'Urgent': '#e74c3c', 'High': '#e67e22', 'Medium': '#f39c12', 'Low': '#2ecc71'}\n",
    "priority_counts = df['priority'].value_counts()\n",
    "bars = axes[0].bar(priority_counts.index, priority_counts.values)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Color each bar\n",
    "for bar, priority in zip(bars, priority_counts.index):\n",
    "    bar.set_color(colors[priority])\n",
    "\n",
    "axes[0].set_title('Email Count by Priority', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
    "axes[0].set_xlabel('Priority', fontsize=12)\n",
    "axes[0].set_ylabel('Number of Emails', fontsize=12)\n",
    "axes[0].grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Add count labels on bars\n",
    "for i, (priority, count) in enumerate(priority_counts.items()):\n",
    "    axes[0].text(i, count + 10, str(count), ha='center', fontweight='bold')\n",
    "\n",
    "# Pie chart\n",
    "pie_colors = [colors[p] for p in priority_counts.index]\n",
    "axes[1].pie(priority_counts.values, labels=priority_counts.index, autopct='%1.1f%%',\n",
    "            colors=pie_colors, startangle=90, textprops={'fontsize': 11, 'fontweight': 'bold'})\n",
    "axes[1].set_title('Priority Distribution', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n⚠️  OBSERVATION: Class Imbalance Detected!\")\n",
    "print(\"   Urgent emails are only ~10% of the data\")\n",
    "print(\"   This means we need to use 'class_weight=balanced' when training\")"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "# Calculate lengths\n",
    "df['subject_length'] = df['subject'].str.len()\n",
    "df['body_length'] = df['body'].str.len()\n",
    "df['word_count'] = df['body'].str.split().str.len()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Text Length Statistics:\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*50)\n",
    "print(df[['subject_length', 'body_length', 'word_count']].describe())\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\\nBy Priority:\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*50)\n",
    "print(df.groupby('priority')[['subject_length', 'body_length', 'word_count']].mean())"
   ]
  },

    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(16, 5))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Box plots for each metric\n",
    "for idx, (column, title) in enumerate([\n",
    "    ('subject_length', 'Subject Length'),\n",
    "    ('body_length', 'Body Length'),\n",
    "    ('word_count', 'Word Count')\n",
    "]):\n",
    "    # Create box plot\n",
    "    df.boxplot(column=column, by='priority', ax=axes[idx])\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_title(title + ' by Priority', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_xlabel('Priority', fontsize=10)\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_ylabel('Count', fontsize=10)\n",
    "    axes[idx].get_figure().suptitle('')  # Remove automatic title\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n📊 OBSERVATION:\")\n",
    "print(\"   Text length alone doesn't strongly distinguish priorities\")\n",
    "print(\"   We need to look at the CONTENT, not just the length!\")"
   ]

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b3a31847-9948-4389-8824-c2517d520155",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Count Urgency Signals"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 24,
   "id": "8da26aff-07a5-4d8f-b0fa-47f91049d7a5",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Analyzing urgency signals in emails...\n",
      "\n",
      "======================================================================\n",
      "AVERAGE URGENCY INDICATORS BY PRIORITY\n",
      "======================================================================\n",
      "          exclamations  caps_words  urgent_words  questions\n",
      "priority                                                   \n",
      "High              0.00       0.000         0.232      0.232\n",
      "Low               0.22       0.184         0.000      0.000\n",
      "Medium            0.00       0.000         0.185      0.565\n",
      "Urgent            1.17       0.610         1.640      0.000\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def count_urgency_signals(text):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Count various urgency indicators in text\"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    text_lower = text.lower()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return {\n",
    "        'exclamations': text.count('!'),\n",
    "        'caps_words': len(re.findall(r'\\b[A-Z]{2,}\\b', text)),\n",
    "        'urgent_words': len(re.findall(\n",
    "            r'\\b(urgent|asap|critical|emergency|immediately|now|help|asap)\\b', \n",
    "            text_lower\n",
    "        )),\n",
    "        'questions': text.count('?')\n",
    "    }\n",
    "\n",
    "# Apply to all emails\n",
    "print(\"Analyzing urgency signals in emails...\")\n",
    "urgency_df = df['body'].apply(count_urgency_signals).apply(pd.Series)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Combine with original data\n",
    "df_analysis = pd.concat([df, urgency_df], axis=1)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Calculate averages by priority\n",
    "urgency_by_priority = df_analysis.groupby('priority')[\n",
    "    ['exclamations', 'caps_words', 'urgent_words', 'questions']\n",
    "].mean()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"AVERAGE URGENCY INDICATORS BY PRIORITY\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "print(urgency_by_priority)"
   ]
  },

      "  improving        57 ███████████████████\n",
      "  user             57 ███████████████████\n",
      "  interface        57 ███████████████████\n",
      "  consider         55 ██████████████████\n",
      "  adding           55 ██████████████████\n",
      "  keyboard         55 ██████████████████\n",
      "  shortcuts        55 ██████████████████\n",
      "  just             55 ██████████████████\n"
     ]

   ],
   "source": [
    "def get_common_words(texts, n=15):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Extract most common meaningful words\"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Combine all texts\n",
    "    all_text = ' '.join(texts).lower()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Extract words (3+ letters, only letters)\n",
    "    words = re.findall(r'\\b[a-z]{3,}\\b', all_text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove common stop words\n",
    "    stop_words = {\n",
    "        'the', 'and', 'for', 'this', 'that', 'with', 'from', \n",
    "        'have', 'are', 'not', 'you', 'can', 'need', 'my'\n",
    "    }\n",
    "    words = [w for w in words if w not in stop_words]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return Counter(words).most_common(n)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"MOST COMMON WORDS BY PRIORITY\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "for priority in ['Urgent', 'High', 'Medium', 'Low']:\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n{'='*70}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"🔍 {priority.upper()} Priority\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'='*70}\")\n",
    "    \n",
    "    priority_texts = df[df['priority'] == priority]['body']\n",
    "    top_words = get_common_words(priority_texts, n=10)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    for word, count in top_words:\n",
    "        bar = '█' * (count // 3)  # Visual bar\n",
    "        print(f\"  {word:15s} {count:3d} {bar}\")"
   ]
  },

    "print(\"Loading data...\")\n",
    "df = pd.read_csv('../data/processed/emails_with_features.csv')\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Loaded {len(df)} emails\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nColumns: {list(df.columns)}\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nShape: {df.shape}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Quick look\n",
    "df.head(3)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "06cb9169-0ada-43b7-8b4b-fabf99f4d719",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## CLean and Prepare text"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "363c7903-0a50-4c13-a2b7-eb2a97fd2643",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Cleaning text...\n",
      "✅ Text cleaned!\n",
      "\n",
      "Example cleaned text:\n",
      "Feature not working properly I am getting an error when I try to export my data. Can you help me fix this?\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def clean_text(text):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Clean text for feature extraction.\n",
    "    \n",
    "    Note: We keep some \"dirty\" elements like !!! and CAPS\n",
    "    because they're actually useful signals for urgency!\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    if pd.isna(text):\n",
    "        return \"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Convert to string (just in case)\n",
    "    text = str(text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove URLs (not useful for priority)\n",
    "    text = re.sub(r'http\\S+|www\\S+', '', text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove email addresses (privacy)\n",
    "    text = re.sub(r'\\S+@\\S+', '', text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove extra whitespace\n",
    "    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return text\n",
    "\n",
    "# Apply cleaning\n",
    "print(\"Cleaning text...\")\n",
    "df['subject_clean'] = df['subject'].apply(clean_text)\n",
    "df['body_clean'] = df['body'].apply(clean_text)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Combine subject and body for full text\n",
    "df['full_text'] = df['subject_clean'] + ' ' + df['body_clean']\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"✅ Text cleaned!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nExample cleaned text:\")\n",
    "print(df['full_text'].iloc[0])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",

   "source": [
    "## TF-IDF example"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "4c8faa1e-4df7-4b80-bf46-9d09206bea5b",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "----------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
      "UNDERSTANDING TF-IDF (Term Frequency - Inverse Document Frequency)\n",
      "----------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
      "\n",
      "📧 Sample emails:\n",
      "   1. urgent help needed now\n",
      "   2. feature request for future\n",
      "   3. urgent critical issue\n",
      "\n",
      "📚 Vocabulary (unique words found):\n",
      "   ['critical' 'feature' 'for' 'future' 'help' 'issue' 'needed' 'now'\n",
      " 'request' 'urgent']\n",
      "\n",
      "🔢 TF-IDF Matrix shape: (3, 10)\n",
      "   (3 emails × 10 words)\n",
      "\n",
      "📊 TF-IDF Values (higher = more important in that email):\n",
      "         critical  feature  for  future      help     issue    needed  \\\n",
      "Email 1  0.000000      0.0  0.0     0.0  0.528635  0.000000  0.528635   \n",
      "Email 2  0.000000      0.5  0.5     0.5  0.000000  0.000000  0.000000   \n",
      "Email 3  0.622766      0.0  0.0     0.0  0.000000  0.622766  0.000000   \n",
      "\n",
      "              now  request   urgent  \n",
      "Email 1  0.528635      0.0  0.40204  \n",
      "Email 2  0.000000      0.5  0.00000  \n",
      "Email 3  0.000000      0.0  0.47363  \n",
      "\n",
      "💡 KEY INSIGHTS:\n",
      "   • 'urgent' has HIGH value in emails 1 & 3 (appears there)\n",
      "   • 'urgent' has ZERO in email 2 (doesn't appear)\n",
      "   • Words appearing in ALL emails get LOWER scores (less distinctive)\n",
      "   • Rare words get HIGHER scores (more distinctive)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"UNDERSTANDING TF-IDF (Term Frequency - Inverse Document Frequency)\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Simple example\n",
    "sample_emails = [\n",
    "    \"urgent help needed now\",\n",
    "    \"feature request for future\",\n",
    "    \"urgent critical issue\",\n",
    "]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n📧 Sample emails:\")\n",
    "for i, email in enumerate(sample_emails, 1):\n",
    "    print(f\"   {i}. {email}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create TF-IDF vectorizer (simple version)\n",
    "vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer()\n",
    "tfidf_matrix = vectorizer.fit_transform(sample_emails)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Show the vocabulary\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📚 Vocabulary (unique words found):\")\n",
    "print(f\"   {vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n🔢 TF-IDF Matrix shape: {tfidf_matrix.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   (3 emails × {len(vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())} words)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Show matrix as DataFrame for readability\n",
    "tfidf_df = pd.DataFrame(\n",
    "    tfidf_matrix.toarray(),\n",
    "    columns=vectorizer.get_feature_names_out(),\n",
    "    index=['Email 1', 'Email 2', 'Email 3']\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 TF-IDF Values (higher = more important in that email):\")\n",
    "print(tfidf_df)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n💡 KEY INSIGHTS:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • 'urgent' has HIGH value in emails 1 & 3 (appears there)\")\n",
    "print(\"   • 'urgent' has ZERO in email 2 (doesn't appear)\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Words appearing in ALL emails get LOWER scores (less distinctive)\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Rare words get HIGHER scores (more distinctive)\")"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "print(\"Extracting TF-IDF features...\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create TF-IDF vectorizer\n",
    "tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(\n",
    "    max_features=300,        # Keep top 300 most important words\n",
    "    min_df=2,                # Word must appear in at least 2 emails\n",
    "    max_df=0.8,              # Ignore words in more than 80% of emails\n",
    "    ngram_range=(1, 2),      # Use single words AND word pairs\n",
    "    stop_words='english',    # Remove common words (the, and, is, etc.)\n",
    "    lowercase=True           # Convert to lowercase for consistency\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Fit on training data and transform\n",
    "tfidf_features = tfidf_vectorizer.fit_transform(df['full_text'])\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ TF-IDF features created!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nFeature matrix shape: {tfidf_features.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   ({tfidf_features.shape[0]} emails × {tfidf_features.shape[1]} features)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Show feature names\n",
    "feature_names = tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📚 Sample feature names (words and word pairs):\")\n",
    "print(f\"   {list(feature_names[:20])}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n💾 Memory used: {tfidf_features.data.nbytes / 1024:.2f} KB (sparse matrix!)\")"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "def get_top_tfidf_words(tfidf_matrix, feature_names, priority_mask, top_n=10):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Get words with highest average TF-IDF score for a priority class\"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Get TF-IDF values for this priority\n",
    "    priority_tfidf = tfidf_matrix[priority_mask.values]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Calculate mean TF-IDF score for each word\n",
    "    mean_tfidf = np.asarray(priority_tfidf.mean(axis=0)).flatten()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Get top words\n",
    "    top_indices = mean_tfidf.argsort()[-top_n:][::-1]\n",
    "    top_words = [(feature_names[i], mean_tfidf[i]) for i in top_indices]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return top_words\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"MOST IMPORTANT WORDS BY PRIORITY (TF-IDF Analysis)\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "for priority in ['Urgent', 'High', 'Medium', 'Low']:\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n🔍 {priority.upper()} Priority:\")\n",
    "    print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    priority_mask = df['priority'] == priority\n",
    "    top_words = get_top_tfidf_words(tfidf_features, feature_names, priority_mask, top_n=15)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    for word, score in top_words:\n",
    "        bar = '█' * int(score * 100)\n",
    "        print(f\"   {word:25s} {score:.4f} {bar}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"💡 OBSERVATION:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Urgent: 'urgent', 'asap', 'critical', 'locked', 'cannot'\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Low: 'suggestion', 'feature', 'would', 'nice'\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Clear vocabulary differences!\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)"
   ]
  },

   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",

      "Urgent       13.8600      72.420         27.5400               0.0  \n"
     ]

   ],
   "source": [
    "def extract_custom_features(df):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Extract hand-crafted features based on our exploration.\n",
    "    These capture signals that TF-IDF might miss!\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    print(\"Extracting custom features...\")\n",
    "    \n",
    "    features = pd.DataFrame()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 1. EXCLAMATION MARKS (urgent emails use more)\n",
    "    features['exclamation_count'] = df['body'].str.count('!')\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 2. QUESTION MARKS (confusion/issues)\n",
    "    features['question_count'] = df['body'].str.count('\\?')\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 3. CAPS WORDS (shouting = urgency)\n",
    "    features['caps_word_count'] = df['body'].apply(\n",
    "        lambda x: len(re.findall(r'\\b[A-Z]{2,}\\b', str(x)))\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 4. URGENT KEYWORDS (explicit urgency)\n",
    "    urgent_pattern = r'\\b(urgent|asap|critical|emergency|immediately|now|help)\\b'\n",
    "    features['urgent_keyword_count'] = df['full_text'].str.lower().str.count(urgent_pattern)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 5. PROBLEM KEYWORDS (indicates issues)\n",
    "    problem_pattern = r'\\b(cannot|can\\'t|unable|broken|error|failed|issue|problem)\\b'\n",
    "    features['problem_keyword_count'] = df['full_text'].str.lower().str.count(problem_pattern)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 6. SENTIMENT (negative = problems)\n",
    "    print(\"   Analyzing sentiment...\")\n",
    "    features['sentiment_polarity'] = df['body_clean'].apply(\n",
    "        lambda x: TextBlob(str(x)).sentiment.polarity\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 7. TEXT LENGTH FEATURES\n",
    "    features['word_count'] = df['body_clean'].str.split().str.len()\n",
    "    features['char_count'] = df['body_clean'].str.len()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 8. SUBJECT LENGTH (short subjects might be urgent)\n",
    "    features['subject_length'] = df['subject_clean'].str.len()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 9. ALL CAPS SUBJECT (indicates urgency)\n",
    "    features['subject_all_caps'] = df['subject'].apply(\n",
    "        lambda x: int(str(x).isupper()) if len(str(x)) > 3 else 0\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    \n",
    "    print(f\"✅ Extracted {len(features.columns)} custom features!\")\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return features\n",
    "\n",
    "# Extract features\n",
    "custom_features = extract_custom_features(df)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 Custom features created:\")\n",
    "print(custom_features.head())\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📈 Custom feature statistics by priority:\")\n",
    "print(custom_features.groupby(df['priority']).mean())"
   ]
  },
  {

   "source": [
    "## Visualize Custom Features"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 11,
   "id": "4e87bab5-056b-4a57-a6bf-66e224c88022",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 1400x1000 with 4 Axes>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "\n",
      "📊 VISUALIZATION INSIGHTS:\n",
      "   • Exclamation marks: Higher in urgent emails\n",
      "   • Urgent keywords: Clear difference between priorities\n",
      "   • Sentiment: More negative in urgent/high priority\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "key_features = [\n",
    "    'exclamation_count',\n",
    "    'urgent_keyword_count',\n",
    "    'problem_keyword_count',\n",
    "    'sentiment_polarity'\n",
    "]\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))\n",
    "axes = axes.flatten()\n",
    "\n",
    "for idx, feature in enumerate(key_features):\n",
    "    # Create DataFrame for plotting\n",
    "    plot_df = pd.DataFrame({\n",
    "        'Priority': df['priority'],\n",
    "        'Value': custom_features[feature]\n",
    "    })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Box plot\n",
    "    plot_df.boxplot(column='Value', by='Priority', ax=axes[idx])\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_title(f'{feature}', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_xlabel('Priority')\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_ylabel('Value')\n",
    "    axes[idx].get_figure().suptitle('')  # Remove automatic title\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n📊 VISUALIZATION INSIGHTS:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Exclamation marks: Higher in urgent emails\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Urgent keywords: Clear difference between priorities\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Sentiment: More negative in urgent/high priority\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "38aaa858-1cf7-4810-b364-a4fc175e1ceb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Combine All Features"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 12,
   "id": "89f4a7f3-ba4d-45c2-9eaf-e3082bf62d82",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Combining TF-IDF and custom features...\n",
      "----------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
      "✅ Combined feature matrix created!\n",
      "\n",
      "Final feature matrix shape: (1000, 310)\n",
      "   • TF-IDF features: 300\n",
      "   • Custom features: 10\n",
      "   • Total features: 310\n",
      "\n",
      "🎯 Target variable (priority):\n",
      "priority\n",
      "Medium    400\n",
      "High      250\n",
      "Low       250\n",
      "Urgent    100\n",
      "Name: count, dtype: int64\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print(\"Combining TF-IDF and custom features...\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Convert custom features to sparse matrix for efficient combination\n",
    "custom_features_sparse = csr_matrix(custom_features.values)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Combine: [TF-IDF features | Custom features]\n",
    "X = hstack([tfidf_features, custom_features_sparse])\n",
    "\n",
    "# Target variable\n",
    "y = df['priority']\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Combined feature matrix created!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nFinal feature matrix shape: {X.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • TF-IDF features: {tfidf_features.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Custom features: {custom_features.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Total features: {X.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n🎯 Target variable (priority):\")\n",
    "print(y.value_counts())"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c5c35e8d-2df5-4485-a35d-ff45f7166e0d",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Train Test Split"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 13,
   "id": "b86be80f-17b6-4f0e-9096-888eadc54f40",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Splitting data into train and test sets...\n",
      "======================================================================\n",
      "✅ Data split complete!\n",
      "\n",
      "📊 Training set:\n",
      "   • Samples: 800\n",
      "   • Features: 310\n",
      "   • Priority distribution:\n",
      "     - Medium  : 320 ( 40.0%)\n",
      "     - Low     : 200 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - High    : 200 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - Urgent  :  80 ( 10.0%)\n",
      "\n",
      "📊 Test set:\n",
      "   • Samples: 200\n",
      "   • Features: 310\n",
      "   • Priority distribution:\n",
      "     - Medium  :  80 ( 40.0%)\n",
      "     - High    :  50 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - Low     :  50 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - Urgent  :  20 ( 10.0%)\n",
      "\n",
      "💡 NOTE: Class distributions match! (thanks to stratify=y)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print(\"Splitting data into train and test sets...\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Split with stratification (keeps class balance)\n",
    "X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(\n",
    "    X, \n",
    "    y,\n",
    "    test_size=0.2,           # 20% for testing\n",
    "    random_state=42,         # For reproducibility\n",
    "    stratify=y               # Keep same class distribution in both sets\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Data split complete!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 Training set:\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Samples: {X_train.shape[0]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Features: {X_train.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Priority distribution:\")\n",
    "for priority, count in y_train.value_counts().items():\n",
    "    percentage = count / len(y_train) * 100\n",
    "    print(f\"     - {priority:8s}: {count:3d} ({percentage:5.1f}%)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 Test set:\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Samples: {X_test.shape[0]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Features: {X_test.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Priority distribution:\")\n",
    "for priority, count in y_test.value_counts().items():\n",
    "    percentage = count / len(y_test) * 100\n",
    "    print(f\"     - {priority:8s}: {count:3d} ({percentage:5.1f}%)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n💡 NOTE: Class distributions match! (thanks to stratify=y)\")"
   ]
  },

    "\n",
    "print(f\"Total features: {len(all_feature_names)}\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\n🔤 Sample TF-IDF features (first 10):\")\n",
    "print(all_feature_names[:10])\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n✨ Custom features (all):\")\n",
    "print(all_feature_names[-len(custom_features.columns):])\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n💡 Why we need feature names:\")\n",

   ]

    "import joblib\n",
    "import json\n",
    "from datetime import datetime\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Saving feature engineering artifacts...\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# 1. Save the TF-IDF vectorizer (need this for new emails!)\n",
    "joblib.dump(tfidf_vectorizer, '../models/tfidf_vectorizer.pkl')\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved TF-IDF vectorizer\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 2. Save feature names\n",
    "with open('../models/feature_names.json', 'w') as f:\n",
    "    json.dump(all_feature_names, f, indent=2)\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved feature names\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 3. Save train-test splits\n",
    "joblib.dump({\n",
    "    'X_train': X_train,\n",
    "    'X_test': X_test,\n",
    "    'y_train': y_train,\n",
    "    'y_test': y_test\n",
    "}, '../models/train_test_data.pkl')\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved train-test data\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 4. Save metadata\n",
    "metadata = {\n",
    "    'created_date': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n",
    "    'n_samples': len(df),\n",
    "    'n_features': X.shape[1],\n",
    "    'n_tfidf_features': tfidf_features.shape[1],\n",
    "    'n_custom_features': custom_features.shape[1],\n",
    "    'train_samples': X_train.shape[0],\n",
    "    'test_samples': X_test.shape[0],\n",
    "    'tfidf_params': {\n",
    "        'max_features': 300,\n",
    "        'min_df': 2,\n",
    "        'max_df': 0.8,\n",
    "        'ngram_range': '(1, 2)',\n",
    "        'stop_words': 'english'\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    'custom_features': list(custom_features.columns)\n",
    "}\n",
    "\n",
    "with open('../models/feature_engineering_metadata.json', 'w') as f:\n",
    "    json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved metadata\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 5. Save processed features DataFrame (optional, for analysis)\n",
    "feature_df = pd.DataFrame(\n",
    "    X.toarray(),\n",
    "    columns=all_feature_names\n",
    ")\n",
    "feature_df['priority'] = y.values\n",
    "feature_df.to_csv('../data/processed/features_complete.csv', index=False)\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved complete feature DataFrame\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"🎉 FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETE!\")\n",

    "print(\"   • models/feature_engineering_metadata.json\")\n",
    "print(\"   • data/processed/features_complete.csv\")"
   ]

    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import seaborn as sns\n",
    "from collections import Counter\n",
    "import re\n",
    "import warnings\n",
    "warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')\n",
    "\n",
    "# Make plots look nice\n",
    "sns.set_style('whitegrid')\n",
    "plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (12, 6)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"✅ Libraries imported successfully!\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "1fe695b7-7caf-4df2-a8a0-b752ccdefd82",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Function to Generate Emails"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "fba15d58-14f4-4601-88c7-395640e362e3",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Generating sample emails...\n",
      "✅ Generated 1000 emails\n",
      "✅ Saved to data/raw/emails.csv\n",
      "\n",
      "Dataset shape: (1000, 4)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def generate_sample_emails(n_samples=1000):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Generate realistic customer support emails.\n",
    "    In a real project, you'd load actual data instead.\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    np.random.seed(42)  # For reproducibility\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for URGENT emails\n",
    "    urgent_subjects = [\n",
    "        'URGENT: Account locked',\n",
    "        'Cannot access my account!!!',\n",
    "        'Payment failed - CRITICAL',\n",
    "        'Security breach detected',\n",
    "        'Lost all my data - HELP',\n",
    "        'EMERGENCY: Service completely down',\n",
    "        'Account hacked - need immediate help',\n",
    "        'CRITICAL ERROR - Cannot login'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    urgent_bodies = [\n",
    "        'I cannot access my account and I have an important meeting in 1 hour. This is extremely urgent! Please help ASAP.',\n",
    "        'My payment failed and now my account is locked. I need this fixed immediately!!!',\n",
    "        'I think my account has been hacked. All my data is gone. URGENT.',\n",
    "        'The entire service is down for my team. We cannot work. This is critical!',\n",
    "        'I lost access to all my files. Need help NOW!',\n",
    "        'CRITICAL: Cannot login and I have a deadline in 30 minutes!',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for HIGH priority\n",
    "    high_subjects = [\n",
    "        'Feature not working properly',\n",
    "        'Error message when exporting',\n",
    "        'Bug in the dashboard',\n",
    "        'Cannot upload files',\n",
    "        'Integration stopped working',\n",
    "        'Getting error code 500'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    high_bodies = [\n",
    "        'I am getting an error when I try to export my data. Can you help me fix this?',\n",
    "        'The reporting feature stopped working. I need this for my weekly report.',\n",
    "        'Files are not uploading correctly. This is affecting my workflow.',\n",
    "        'Getting an error code when I try to access the dashboard.',\n",
    "        'The integration with Slack is broken. Please look into this.',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for MEDIUM priority\n",
    "    medium_subjects = [\n",
    "        'Question about billing',\n",
    "        'How to use advanced features?',\n",
    "        'Need clarification on pricing',\n",
    "        'Setup assistance needed',\n",
    "        'Best practices question',\n",
    "        'How do I configure this?'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    medium_bodies = [\n",
    "        'I have a question about how the billing works for multiple users.',\n",
    "        'Can you explain how to set up the advanced filters? Thanks.',\n",
    "        'I need some clarification on the different pricing tiers.',\n",
    "        'What is the best way to organize my projects in the system?',\n",
    "        'Could you help me understand how the permissions work?',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Patterns for LOW priority\n",
    "    low_subjects = [\n",
    "        'Feature suggestion',\n",
    "        'Feedback on recent update',\n",
    "        'Enhancement idea',\n",
    "        'Would be nice to have...',\n",
    "        'General feedback',\n",
    "        'Suggestion for improvement'\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    low_bodies = [\n",
    "        'It would be great if you could add a dark mode option in the future.',\n",
    "        'I have a suggestion for improving the user interface.',\n",
    "        'Overall the product is good. Here is some feedback for consideration.',\n",
    "        'Would be nice to have an export to PDF feature someday.',\n",
    "        'Consider adding keyboard shortcuts. Just a suggestion!',\n",
    "    ]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Create emails with realistic distribution\n",
    "    # Real customer support: 10% Urgent, 25% High, 40% Medium, 25% Low\n",
    "    \n",
    "    emails = []\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate URGENT emails (10%)\n",
    "    n_urgent = int(n_samples * 0.10)\n",
    "    for i in range(n_urgent):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': i + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(urgent_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(urgent_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'Urgent'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate HIGH priority emails (25%)\n",
    "    n_high = int(n_samples * 0.25)\n",
    "    for i in range(n_high):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': len(emails) + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(high_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(high_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'High'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate MEDIUM priority emails (40%)\n",
    "    n_medium = int(n_samples * 0.40)\n",
    "    for i in range(n_medium):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': len(emails) + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(medium_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(medium_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'Medium'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Generate LOW priority emails (25%)\n",
    "    n_low = n_samples - len(emails)  # Remaining\n",
    "    for i in range(n_low):\n",
    "        emails.append({\n",
    "            'email_id': len(emails) + 1,\n",
    "            'subject': np.random.choice(low_subjects),\n",
    "            'body': np.random.choice(low_bodies),\n",
    "            'priority': 'Low'\n",
    "        })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Shuffle so they're not ordered by priority\n",
    "    np.random.shuffle(emails)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Re-assign email IDs after shuffling\n",
    "    for i, email in enumerate(emails):\n",
    "        email['email_id'] = i + 1\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return pd.DataFrame(emails)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Generate the data\n",
    "print(\"Generating sample emails...\")\n",
    "df = generate_sample_emails(1000)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Save it\n",
    "df.to_csv('../data/raw/emails.csv', index=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Generated {len(df)} emails\")\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Saved to data/raw/emails.csv\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nDataset shape: {df.shape}\")"
   ]
  },

    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n⚠️  OBSERVATION: Class Imbalance Detected!\")\n",
    "print(\"   Urgent emails are only ~10% of the data\")\n",
    "print(\"   This means we need to use 'class_weight=balanced' when training\")"
   ]

   ],
   "source": [
    "# Calculate lengths\n",
    "df['subject_length'] = df['subject'].str.len()\n",
    "df['body_length'] = df['body'].str.len()\n",
    "df['word_count'] = df['body'].str.split().str.len()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Text Length Statistics:\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*50)\n",
    "print(df[['subject_length', 'body_length', 'word_count']].describe())\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\\nBy Priority:\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*50)\n",
    "print(df.groupby('priority')[['subject_length', 'body_length', 'word_count']].mean())"
   ]
  },

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b3a31847-9948-4389-8824-c2517d520155",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Count Urgency Signals"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 14,
   "id": "8da26aff-07a5-4d8f-b0fa-47f91049d7a5",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Analyzing urgency signals in emails...\n",
      "\n",
      "======================================================================\n",
      "AVERAGE URGENCY INDICATORS BY PRIORITY\n",
      "======================================================================\n",
      "          exclamations  caps_words  urgent_words  questions\n",
      "priority                                                   \n",
      "High              0.00       0.000         0.232      0.232\n",
      "Low               0.22       0.184         0.000      0.000\n",
      "Medium            0.00       0.000         0.185      0.565\n",
      "Urgent            1.17       0.610         1.640      0.000\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def count_urgency_signals(text):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Count various urgency indicators in text\"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    text_lower = text.lower()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return {\n",
    "        'exclamations': text.count('!'),\n",
    "        'caps_words': len(re.findall(r'\\b[A-Z]{2,}\\b', text)),\n",
    "        'urgent_words': len(re.findall(\n",
    "            r'\\b(urgent|asap|critical|emergency|immediately|now|help|asap)\\b', \n",
    "            text_lower\n",
    "        )),\n",
    "        'questions': text.count('?')\n",
    "    }\n",
    "\n",
    "# Apply to all emails\n",
    "print(\"Analyzing urgency signals in emails...\")\n",
    "urgency_df = df['body'].apply(count_urgency_signals).apply(pd.Series)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Combine with original data\n",
    "df_analysis = pd.concat([df, urgency_df], axis=1)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Calculate averages by priority\n",
    "urgency_by_priority = df_analysis.groupby('priority')[\n",
    "    ['exclamations', 'caps_words', 'urgent_words', 'questions']\n",
    "].mean()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"=\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"AVERAGE URGENCY INDICATORS BY PRIORITY\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "print(urgency_by_priority)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "5e828174-dfdb-4ca3-a80d-524c435732b7",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Visulize Urgency Indicator"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 15,
   "id": "93ad777d-73b7-4370-bc6d-db5ec5049165",

   "source": [
    "def get_common_words(texts, n=15):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Extract most common meaningful words\"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Combine all texts\n",
    "    all_text = ' '.join(texts).lower()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Extract words (3+ letters, only letters)\n",
    "    words = re.findall(r'\\b[a-z]{3,}\\b', all_text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove common stop words\n",
    "    stop_words = {\n",
    "        'the', 'and', 'for', 'this', 'that', 'with', 'from', \n",
    "        'have', 'are', 'not', 'you', 'can', 'need', 'my'\n",
    "    }\n",
    "    words = [w for w in words if w not in stop_words]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return Counter(words).most_common(n)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"MOST COMMON WORDS BY PRIORITY\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "for priority in ['Urgent', 'High', 'Medium', 'Low']:\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n{'='*70}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"🔍 {priority.upper()} Priority\")\n",
    "    print(f\"{'='*70}\")\n",
    "    \n",
    "    priority_texts = df[df['priority'] == priority]['body']\n",
    "    top_words = get_common_words(priority_texts, n=10)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    for word, count in top_words:\n",
    "        bar = '█' * (count // 3)  # Visual bar\n",
    "        print(f\"  {word:15s} {count:3d} {bar}\")"
   ]
  },

    "print(\"Loading data...\")\n",

    "print(f\"\\nColumns: {list(df.columns)}\")\n",

    "# Quick look\n",

   ]

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "06cb9169-0ada-43b7-8b4b-fabf99f4d719",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## CLean and Prepare text"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 20,
   "id": "363c7903-0a50-4c13-a2b7-eb2a97fd2643",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Cleaning text...\n",
      "✅ Text cleaned!\n",
      "\n",
      "Example cleaned text:\n",
      "Feature not working properly I am getting an error when I try to export my data. Can you help me fix this?\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def clean_text(text):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Clean text for feature extraction.\n",
    "    \n",
    "    Note: We keep some \"dirty\" elements like !!! and CAPS\n",
    "    because they're actually useful signals for urgency!\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    if pd.isna(text):\n",
    "        return \"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Convert to string (just in case)\n",
    "    text = str(text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove URLs (not useful for priority)\n",
    "    text = re.sub(r'http\\S+|www\\S+', '', text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove email addresses (privacy)\n",
    "    text = re.sub(r'\\S+@\\S+', '', text)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Remove extra whitespace\n",
    "    text = re.sub(r'\\s+', ' ', text).strip()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return text\n",
    "\n",
    "# Apply cleaning\n",
    "print(\"Cleaning text...\")\n",
    "df['subject_clean'] = df['subject'].apply(clean_text)\n",
    "df['body_clean'] = df['body'].apply(clean_text)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Combine subject and body for full text\n",
    "df['full_text'] = df['subject_clean'] + ' ' + df['body_clean']\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"✅ Text cleaned!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nExample cleaned text:\")\n",
    "print(df['full_text'].iloc[0])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "335ca008-1bbf-482a-8c48-dfacd19050a9",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## TF-IDF example"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 21,
   "id": "4c8faa1e-4df7-4b80-bf46-9d09206bea5b",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "----------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
      "UNDERSTANDING TF-IDF (Term Frequency - Inverse Document Frequency)\n",
      "----------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
      "\n",
      "📧 Sample emails:\n",
      "   1. urgent help needed now\n",
      "   2. feature request for future\n",
      "   3. urgent critical issue\n",
      "\n",
      "📚 Vocabulary (unique words found):\n",
      "   ['critical' 'feature' 'for' 'future' 'help' 'issue' 'needed' 'now'\n",
      " 'request' 'urgent']\n",
      "\n",
      "🔢 TF-IDF Matrix shape: (3, 10)\n",
      "   (3 emails × 10 words)\n",
      "\n",
      "📊 TF-IDF Values (higher = more important in that email):\n",
      "         critical  feature  for  future      help     issue    needed  \\\n",
      "Email 1  0.000000      0.0  0.0     0.0  0.528635  0.000000  0.528635   \n",
      "Email 2  0.000000      0.5  0.5     0.5  0.000000  0.000000  0.000000   \n",
      "Email 3  0.622766      0.0  0.0     0.0  0.000000  0.622766  0.000000   \n",
      "\n",
      "              now  request   urgent  \n",
      "Email 1  0.528635      0.0  0.40204  \n",
      "Email 2  0.000000      0.5  0.00000  \n",
      "Email 3  0.000000      0.0  0.47363  \n",
      "\n",
      "💡 KEY INSIGHTS:\n",
      "   • 'urgent' has HIGH value in emails 1 & 3 (appears there)\n",
      "   • 'urgent' has ZERO in email 2 (doesn't appear)\n",
      "   • Words appearing in ALL emails get LOWER scores (less distinctive)\n",
      "   • Rare words get HIGHER scores (more distinctive)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"UNDERSTANDING TF-IDF (Term Frequency - Inverse Document Frequency)\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Simple example\n",
    "sample_emails = [\n",
    "    \"urgent help needed now\",\n",
    "    \"feature request for future\",\n",
    "    \"urgent critical issue\",\n",
    "]\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n📧 Sample emails:\")\n",
    "for i, email in enumerate(sample_emails, 1):\n",
    "    print(f\"   {i}. {email}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create TF-IDF vectorizer (simple version)\n",
    "vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer()\n",
    "tfidf_matrix = vectorizer.fit_transform(sample_emails)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Show the vocabulary\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📚 Vocabulary (unique words found):\")\n",
    "print(f\"   {vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n🔢 TF-IDF Matrix shape: {tfidf_matrix.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   (3 emails × {len(vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())} words)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Show matrix as DataFrame for readability\n",
    "tfidf_df = pd.DataFrame(\n",
    "    tfidf_matrix.toarray(),\n",
    "    columns=vectorizer.get_feature_names_out(),\n",
    "    index=['Email 1', 'Email 2', 'Email 3']\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 TF-IDF Values (higher = more important in that email):\")\n",
    "print(tfidf_df)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n💡 KEY INSIGHTS:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • 'urgent' has HIGH value in emails 1 & 3 (appears there)\")\n",
    "print(\"   • 'urgent' has ZERO in email 2 (doesn't appear)\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Words appearing in ALL emails get LOWER scores (less distinctive)\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Rare words get HIGHER scores (more distinctive)\")"
   ]
  },

    "## Extra TF-IDF Features"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",

   "id": "93be10d2-8b63-4175-9078-82f7ea33ecbf",

   "source": [
    "print(\"Extracting TF-IDF features...\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Create TF-IDF vectorizer\n",
    "tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(\n",
    "    max_features=300,        # Keep top 300 most important words\n",
    "    min_df=2,                # Word must appear in at least 2 emails\n",
    "    max_df=0.8,              # Ignore words in more than 80% of emails\n",
    "    ngram_range=(1, 2),      # Use single words AND word pairs\n",
    "    stop_words='english',    # Remove common words (the, and, is, etc.)\n",
    "    lowercase=True           # Convert to lowercase for consistency\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Fit on training data and transform\n",
    "tfidf_features = tfidf_vectorizer.fit_transform(df['full_text'])\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ TF-IDF features created!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nFeature matrix shape: {tfidf_features.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   ({tfidf_features.shape[0]} emails × {tfidf_features.shape[1]} features)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Show feature names\n",
    "feature_names = tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📚 Sample feature names (words and word pairs):\")\n",
    "print(f\"   {list(feature_names[:20])}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n💾 Memory used: {tfidf_features.data.nbytes / 1024:.2f} KB (sparse matrix!)\")"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "def get_top_tfidf_words(tfidf_matrix, feature_names, priority_mask, top_n=10):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Get words with highest average TF-IDF score for a priority class\"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Get TF-IDF values for this priority\n",
    "    priority_tfidf = tfidf_matrix[priority_mask.values]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Calculate mean TF-IDF score for each word\n",
    "    mean_tfidf = np.asarray(priority_tfidf.mean(axis=0)).flatten()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Get top words\n",
    "    top_indices = mean_tfidf.argsort()[-top_n:][::-1]\n",
    "    top_words = [(feature_names[i], mean_tfidf[i]) for i in top_indices]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return top_words\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"MOST IMPORTANT WORDS BY PRIORITY (TF-IDF Analysis)\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "for priority in ['Urgent', 'High', 'Medium', 'Low']:\n",
    "    print(f\"\\n🔍 {priority.upper()} Priority:\")\n",
    "    print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    priority_mask = df['priority'] == priority\n",
    "    top_words = get_top_tfidf_words(tfidf_features, feature_names, priority_mask, top_n=15)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    for word, score in top_words:\n",
    "        bar = '█' * int(score * 100)\n",
    "        print(f\"   {word:25s} {score:.4f} {bar}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"💡 OBSERVATION:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Urgent: 'urgent', 'asap', 'critical', 'locked', 'cannot'\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Low: 'suggestion', 'feature', 'would', 'nice'\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Clear vocabulary differences!\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)"
   ]
  },

   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 24,
   "id": "11824b21-e780-473c-99e0-bfbe6e2e451c",

      "\n",

      "priority                                                            \n",
      "High         12.4280      66.664         24.0400               0.0  \n",
      "Low          10.4480      59.280         20.6920               0.0  \n",
      "Medium       10.5825      58.965         24.9025               0.0  \n",
      "Urgent       13.8600      72.420         27.5400               0.0  \n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def extract_custom_features(df):\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    Extract hand-crafted features based on our exploration.\n",
    "    These capture signals that TF-IDF might miss!\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "    \n",
    "    print(\"Extracting custom features...\")\n",
    "    \n",
    "    features = pd.DataFrame()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 1. EXCLAMATION MARKS (urgent emails use more)\n",
    "    features['exclamation_count'] = df['body'].str.count('!')\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 2. QUESTION MARKS (confusion/issues)\n",
    "    features['question_count'] = df['body'].str.count('\\?')\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 3. CAPS WORDS (shouting = urgency)\n",
    "    features['caps_word_count'] = df['body'].apply(\n",
    "        lambda x: len(re.findall(r'\\b[A-Z]{2,}\\b', str(x)))\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 4. URGENT KEYWORDS (explicit urgency)\n",
    "    urgent_pattern = r'\\b(urgent|asap|critical|emergency|immediately|now|help)\\b'\n",
    "    features['urgent_keyword_count'] = df['full_text'].str.lower().str.count(urgent_pattern)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 5. PROBLEM KEYWORDS (indicates issues)\n",
    "    problem_pattern = r'\\b(cannot|can\\'t|unable|broken|error|failed|issue|problem)\\b'\n",
    "    features['problem_keyword_count'] = df['full_text'].str.lower().str.count(problem_pattern)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 6. SENTIMENT (negative = problems)\n",
    "    print(\"   Analyzing sentiment...\")\n",
    "    features['sentiment_polarity'] = df['body_clean'].apply(\n",
    "        lambda x: TextBlob(str(x)).sentiment.polarity\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 7. TEXT LENGTH FEATURES\n",
    "    features['word_count'] = df['body_clean'].str.split().str.len()\n",
    "    features['char_count'] = df['body_clean'].str.len()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 8. SUBJECT LENGTH (short subjects might be urgent)\n",
    "    features['subject_length'] = df['subject_clean'].str.len()\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # 9. ALL CAPS SUBJECT (indicates urgency)\n",
    "    features['subject_all_caps'] = df['subject'].apply(\n",
    "        lambda x: int(str(x).isupper()) if len(str(x)) > 3 else 0\n",
    "    )\n",
    "    \n",
    "    print(f\"✅ Extracted {len(features.columns)} custom features!\")\n",
    "    \n",
    "    return features\n",
    "\n",
    "# Extract features\n",
    "custom_features = extract_custom_features(df)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 Custom features created:\")\n",
    "print(custom_features.head())\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📈 Custom feature statistics by priority:\")\n",
    "print(custom_features.groupby(df['priority']).mean())"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "key_features = [\n",
    "    'exclamation_count',\n",
    "    'urgent_keyword_count',\n",
    "    'problem_keyword_count',\n",
    "    'sentiment_polarity'\n",
    "]\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))\n",
    "axes = axes.flatten()\n",
    "\n",
    "for idx, feature in enumerate(key_features):\n",
    "    # Create DataFrame for plotting\n",
    "    plot_df = pd.DataFrame({\n",
    "        'Priority': df['priority'],\n",
    "        'Value': custom_features[feature]\n",
    "    })\n",
    "    \n",
    "    # Box plot\n",
    "    plot_df.boxplot(column='Value', by='Priority', ax=axes[idx])\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_title(f'{feature}', fontsize=12, fontweight='bold')\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_xlabel('Priority')\n",
    "    axes[idx].set_ylabel('Value')\n",
    "    axes[idx].get_figure().suptitle('')  # Remove automatic title\n",
    "\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n📊 VISUALIZATION INSIGHTS:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Exclamation marks: Higher in urgent emails\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Urgent keywords: Clear difference between priorities\")\n",
    "print(\"   • Sentiment: More negative in urgent/high priority\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "38aaa858-1cf7-4810-b364-a4fc175e1ceb",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Combine All Features"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 26,
   "id": "89f4a7f3-ba4d-45c2-9eaf-e3082bf62d82",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Combining TF-IDF and custom features...\n",
      "----------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
      "✅ Combined feature matrix created!\n",
      "\n",
      "Final feature matrix shape: (1000, 310)\n",
      "   • TF-IDF features: 300\n",
      "   • Custom features: 10\n",
      "   • Total features: 310\n",
      "\n",
      "🎯 Target variable (priority):\n",
      "priority\n",
      "Medium    400\n",
      "High      250\n",

     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print(\"Combining TF-IDF and custom features...\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Convert custom features to sparse matrix for efficient combination\n",
    "custom_features_sparse = csr_matrix(custom_features.values)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Combine: [TF-IDF features | Custom features]\n",
    "X = hstack([tfidf_features, custom_features_sparse])\n",
    "\n",
    "# Target variable\n",
    "y = df['priority']\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Combined feature matrix created!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\nFinal feature matrix shape: {X.shape}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • TF-IDF features: {tfidf_features.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Custom features: {custom_features.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Total features: {X.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n🎯 Target variable (priority):\")\n",
    "print(y.value_counts())"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c5c35e8d-2df5-4485-a35d-ff45f7166e0d",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Train Test Split"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 27,
   "id": "b86be80f-17b6-4f0e-9096-888eadc54f40",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Splitting data into train and test sets...\n",
      "======================================================================\n",
      "✅ Data split complete!\n",
      "\n",
      "📊 Training set:\n",
      "   • Samples: 800\n",
      "   • Features: 310\n",
      "   • Priority distribution:\n",
      "     - Medium  : 320 ( 40.0%)\n",
      "     - Low     : 200 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - High    : 200 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - Urgent  :  80 ( 10.0%)\n",
      "\n",
      "📊 Test set:\n",
      "   • Samples: 200\n",
      "   • Features: 310\n",
      "   • Priority distribution:\n",
      "     - Medium  :  80 ( 40.0%)\n",
      "     - High    :  50 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - Low     :  50 ( 25.0%)\n",
      "     - Urgent  :  20 ( 10.0%)\n",
      "\n",
      "💡 NOTE: Class distributions match! (thanks to stratify=y)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "print(\"Splitting data into train and test sets...\")\n",
    "print(\"=\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Split with stratification (keeps class balance)\n",
    "X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(\n",
    "    X, \n",
    "    y,\n",
    "    test_size=0.2,           # 20% for testing\n",
    "    random_state=42,         # For reproducibility\n",
    "    stratify=y               # Keep same class distribution in both sets\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"✅ Data split complete!\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 Training set:\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Samples: {X_train.shape[0]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Features: {X_train.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Priority distribution:\")\n",
    "for priority, count in y_train.value_counts().items():\n",
    "    percentage = count / len(y_train) * 100\n",
    "    print(f\"     - {priority:8s}: {count:3d} ({percentage:5.1f}%)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n📊 Test set:\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Samples: {X_test.shape[0]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Features: {X_test.shape[1]}\")\n",
    "print(f\"   • Priority distribution:\")\n",
    "for priority, count in y_test.value_counts().items():\n",
    "    percentage = count / len(y_test) * 100\n",
    "    print(f\"     - {priority:8s}: {count:3d} ({percentage:5.1f}%)\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n💡 NOTE: Class distributions match! (thanks to stratify=y)\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "70fae659-c918-43b8-b2c3-7a2044debbc5",

   "source": [
    "## Create Feature Names List"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 28,
   "id": "7cd49fc4-4290-4141-a694-c1e4a5451c92",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Total features: 310\n",
      "\n",
      "🔤 Sample TF-IDF features (first 10):\n",
      "['30', '30 minutes', '500', '500 integration', 'access', 'access account', 'access dashboard', 'access files', 'account', 'account hacked']\n",
      "\n",
      "✨ Custom features (all):\n",
      "['exclamation_count', 'question_count', 'caps_word_count', 'urgent_keyword_count', 'problem_keyword_count', 'sentiment_polarity', 'word_count', 'char_count', 'subject_length', 'subject_all_caps']\n",
      "\n",
      "💡 Why we need feature names:\n",
      "   • To understand which features are most important\n",
      "   • To explain model predictions\n",
      "   • To debug if model isn't working well\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "all_feature_names = (\n",
    "    list(tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out()) + \n",
    "    list(custom_features.columns)\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Total features: {len(all_feature_names)}\")\n",
    "print(f\"\\n🔤 Sample TF-IDF features (first 10):\")\n",
    "print(all_feature_names[:10])\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"\\n✨ Custom features (all):\")\n",
    "print(all_feature_names[-len(custom_features.columns):])\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n💡 Why we need feature names:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • To understand which features are most important\")\n",
    "print(\"   • To explain model predictions\")\n",
    "print(\"   • To debug if model isn't working well\")"
   ]
  },

   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 29,
   "id": "4336d684-57ab-445c-8456-573182f8b3a5",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Saving feature engineering artifacts...\n",
      "----------------------------------------------------------------------\n",
      "✅ Saved TF-IDF vectorizer\n",
      "✅ Saved feature names\n",

      "   • data/processed/features_complete.csv\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import joblib\n",
    "import json\n",
    "from datetime import datetime\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Saving feature engineering artifacts...\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "\n",
    "# 1. Save the TF-IDF vectorizer (need this for new emails!)\n",
    "joblib.dump(tfidf_vectorizer, '../models/tfidf_vectorizer.pkl')\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved TF-IDF vectorizer\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 2. Save feature names\n",
    "with open('../models/feature_names.json', 'w') as f:\n",
    "    json.dump(all_feature_names, f, indent=2)\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved feature names\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 3. Save train-test splits\n",
    "joblib.dump({\n",
    "    'X_train': X_train,\n",
    "    'X_test': X_test,\n",
    "    'y_train': y_train,\n",
    "    'y_test': y_test\n",
    "}, '../models/train_test_data.pkl')\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved train-test data\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 4. Save metadata\n",
    "metadata = {\n",
    "    'created_date': datetime.now().isoformat(),\n",
    "    'n_samples': len(df),\n",
    "    'n_features': X.shape[1],\n",
    "    'n_tfidf_features': tfidf_features.shape[1],\n",
    "    'n_custom_features': custom_features.shape[1],\n",
    "    'train_samples': X_train.shape[0],\n",
    "    'test_samples': X_test.shape[0],\n",
    "    'tfidf_params': {\n",
    "        'max_features': 300,\n",
    "        'min_df': 2,\n",
    "        'max_df': 0.8,\n",
    "        'ngram_range': '(1, 2)',\n",
    "        'stop_words': 'english'\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    'custom_features': list(custom_features.columns)\n",
    "}\n",
    "\n",
    "with open('../models/feature_engineering_metadata.json', 'w') as f:\n",
    "    json.dump(metadata, f, indent=2)\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved metadata\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# 5. Save processed features DataFrame (optional, for analysis)\n",
    "feature_df = pd.DataFrame(\n",
    "    X.toarray(),\n",
    "    columns=all_feature_names\n",
    ")\n",
    "feature_df['priority'] = y.values\n",
    "feature_df.to_csv('../data/processed/features_complete.csv', index=False)\n",
    "print(\"✅ Saved complete feature DataFrame\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"\\n\" + \"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"🎉 FEATURE ENGINEERING COMPLETE!\")\n",
    "print(\"-\"*70)\n",
    "print(\"\\n📦 Saved files:\")\n",
    "print(\"   • models/tfidf_vectorizer.pkl\")\n",
    "print(\"   • models/feature_names.json\")\n",
    "print(\"   • models/train_test_data.pkl\")\n",
    "print(\"   • models/feature_engineering_metadata.json\")\n",
    "print(\"   • data/processed/features_complete.csv\")"
   ]
  },
